title: Shankara Ashtakam
intro: A divine eight-verse hymn dedicated to Lord Shiva, praising his transcendental form and eternal nature.
description:
The Shankara Ashtakam is a profound devotional composition celebrating Lord Shiva's cosmic form and spiritual significance. It is believed to bestow peace, liberation, and divine grace upon the reciter.

|| Verse 1 ||
Nilakantham Mahakantham, Shambhum Bhava Bhayapahm.
Shiva Shankara Deshikam, Bhakta Bhaya Vinaashinm.

Regular recitation brings inner peace and spiritual transformation.
meaning: The Shankara Ashtakam honors Lord Shiva as the supreme consciousness and destroyer of ignorance. It seeks liberation from the cycle of birth and death.

faq.a1: Shankara means 'the auspicious one', a name of Lord Shiva who brings prosperity and well-being.
faq.q2: What is the best time to recite?
faq.a2: Mondays and during full moon nights are considered auspicious for Shiva worship.
faq.q3: What spiritual benefits does it offer?
faq.a3: Inner peace, freedom from fear, and progress toward spiritual liberation.
